title: Cookie Monster
description: A login portal is storing session tokens in plaintext cookies. The admin token was spotted during a traffic dump. Decode the base64 value: `YWRtaW46c2VjcmV0MTIz` to extract the credentials.
hint: Base64 decode the cookie value. The format is username:password.

title: SQL Injection 101
description: The NexusCorp employee portal uses a vulnerable login form. The SQL query is: `SELECT * FROM users WHERE username='$input' AND password='$pass'`. Craft an injection payload to bypass authentication.
hint: Think about how to make the WHERE clause always return true by injecting into the username field.

title: Header Injection
description: A NexusCorp API endpoint reads the X-Forwarded-For header to whitelist internal IPs. The server only allows requests from `192.168.1.0/24`. You need to spoof your source IP to gain access to the admin endpoint.
hint: HTTP headers can be manipulated. The server trusts the X-Forwarded-For header without validation.

title: XSS Payload
description: A user comment field on the NexusCorp status page reflects input without sanitization. Craft an XSS payload that would execute `alert(document.cookie)` to demonstrate cookie theft.
hint: Inject a script tag directly into the comment field. The page reflects input unsanitized.
